realized.security_id: Reportable Income and YTD Reportable Income Adjustment Fields will not be subject to taxation in exempt accounts.
2 The loss from the original closing transaction that must be added to the cost basis of the replacement or newly acquired shares of security that triggered a wash sale event.
AVG - indicates that the average cost methodology is being used to determine cost basis.
Various - indicates that there are multiple tax lots that represent trade dates 5 years or greater.

i. Generally, securities acquired before 2011 in reportable accounts, in retirement accounts or held by Non-U.S. entities are not subject to the cost basis reporting rules set forth in the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended by the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008, and are marked as "noncovered". Securities marked as "covered" were identified as securities potentially subject to the cost basis reporting rules and may be reported to the IRS on form 1099-B for the applicable tax year in which the securities are disposed.

ii. Pershing shall not be responsible for and makes no representations or warranties with respect to the accuracy of any information that you report to the IRS or other taxing authorities, and accordingly, disclaims any and all liability that may arise with respect to your use and reliance on the information provided herein for such reporting.
iii. * Please contact your Financial Organization and/or investment professional to provide cost basis information for this tax lot.
iv. Alternative Investment cost basis fields generally are inaccurate and therefore will not be displayed. Please contact issuer for K1 information

This information displayed above is being generated as a courtesy and is for informational purposes only and is not intended, in any manner, as an official brokerage or mutual fund statement. This information is not to be used as an official books and records statement.
